name: Source Fidelity review for Circulating Factor Modulation bundle
summary: Most staged source, study, and finding records match the cited PubMed and PMC source boundaries: animal parabiosis, cord-plasma, and plasma-dilution claims stay preclinical; TPE claims in adults over 50 stay biomarker-level; PLASMA is framed as feasibility and safety; and AMBAR is framed as Alzheimer disease-specific functional evidence. One staged source record needs revision because the PMID 40424097 title is paraphrased rather than the PubMed title.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged source name for PMID 40424097 is a paraphrase, not the PubMed article title.
findings.why_it_matters: Source records are reused as public bibliographic anchors. A nonmatching title makes later source inspection harder and weakens source fidelity even though the PMID, DOI, registry ID, and evidence interpretation are otherwise aligned.
findings.recommended_action: Change the staged source name to the PubMed title: Multi-Omics Analysis Reveals Biomarkers That Contribute to Biological Age Rejuvenation in Response to Single-Blinded Randomized Placebo-Controlled Therapeutic Plasma Exchange.

findings.claim_or_issue: The source set mixes animal functional evidence, human surrogate biomarker evidence, a small young-plasma Alzheimer feasibility study, and a disease-specific AMBAR functional trial.
findings.why_it_matters: These source classes support different evidence tiers and should not be merged into a broad healthy-aging or LEV-relevant functional claim.
findings.recommended_action: Retain the staged caveats that keep animal effects preclinical, TPE aging signals biomarker-level, PLASMA non-efficacy-oriented, and AMBAR Alzheimer-specific.
